description: Goal: restore the layer split in compiler2 source handling. ScopeCode should only publish what exists at module scope; ordinary function bodies should remain raw quoted source until that function is demanded. Signal: source publication no longer waits on body-local macro readiness for ordinary functions; a staged function-local expansion fact gates DefineFunction; item-level expansion remains eager because it can mutate sibling scope; direct source and Fz.Compiler.define publish identical raw definition facts; docs and telemetry state the split explicitly. Strategy: first introduce staged expanded-function facts and teach DefineFunction to demand them, then delete eager body expansion from source_publish while keeping item-level expansion, then rebuild direct and compiler-service publication around raw definition transactions and close out the old eager paths.
title: Compiler2 function staging: move function-body expansion behind demand
